title: Werauhia gibba
taxonomy: Werauhia gibba is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Werauhia gibba places it within the genus Werauhia, which is part of the Bromelioideae subfamily. The Bromelioideae subfamily is further classified under the family Bromeliaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as air plants or tillandsias. This classification is based on the plant's distinctive characteristics, such as its epiphytic growth habit and modified leaves that form a cup-like structure.

intro: Werauhia gibba, also known as Tillandsia gibba, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its unique, bulbous structure and ability to thrive in dry conditions. The health benefits of Werauhia gibba include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
